content.definition: To stop sleeping or to cause someone to stop sleeping.
examples: I always wake up before my alarm.
Can you wake me up at 7?
The noise woke up the baby.
content.relatedDefinition: To get out of bed after waking up.
relatedExamples: I woke up at 6 but didn't get up until 7.
Get up, it's time for school!
She gets up early to exercise.

content.definition: To place clothes on your body or to turn on a device.
examples: Put on your coat, it's cold outside.
She put on her glasses to read.
Can you put on some music?
content.relatedDefinition: To remove clothes from your body or to leave the ground (plane).
relatedExamples: Take off your shoes before entering.
The plane took off on time.
He took off his hat inside the building.

content.instructions: Choose the correct phrasal verb for each sentence.

questions.sentence: I ___ at 6 AM every day, but stay in bed until 6:30.
questions.explanation: 'Wake up' means to stop sleeping; 'get up' is to leave bed.

questions.sentence: It's raining! You should ___ a jacket.
questions.explanation: 'Put on' means to place clothing on your body.

questions.sentence: Please ___ your shoes at the door.
On Saturdays I don't ___ until noon.
questions.explanation: 'Get up' means to get out of bed.

content.definition: To start a machine, light, or device.
examples: Turn on the TV, the match is starting.
Can you turn on the heater? It's cold.
She turned on her laptop to check emails.
content.relatedDefinition: To stop a machine, light, or device.
relatedExamples: Turn off the lights when you leave.
Don't forget to turn off the oven!
He turned off his phone during the meeting.

content.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct phrasal verb.

questions.sentence: It's dark in here. Can you ___ the lights?
questions.explanation: 'Turn on' means to start a device or light.

questions.sentence: Please ___ the TV, I'm trying to study.
questions.explanation: 'Turn off' means to stop a device.

questions.sentence: The alarm clock ___ me ___ every morning at 7.
questions.explanation: 'Wake up' can be separated: 'wakes me up'.

questions.sentence: It's warm inside, you can ___ your scarf.

content.instructions: Final review of all daily routine phrasal verbs.

questions.sentence: My neighbour's dog ___ me ___ at 5 AM.
questions.explanation: 'Wake up' (separable) means to cause to stop sleeping.

questions.sentence: Don't forget to ___ the stove before leaving.
questions.explanation: 'Turn off' the stove means to switch it off for safety.

questions.sentence: She ___ her running shoes and went for a jog.
questions.explanation: 'Put on' means to wear or place on one's body.

questions.sentence: On holidays, I like to ___ late and relax.
questions.explanation: 'Get up late' means to leave bed late, implying relaxation.
